Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement real H_eff with MPO application (CPU version)

Replaced placeholder identity operation with full 4-step H_eff contraction:
1. T1 = L_env * theta (contract left MPS bond)
2. T2 = W_left * T1 (apply left MPO)
3. T3 = W_right * T2 (apply right MPO)
4. result = T3 * R_env (contract right MPS bond)

IMPLEMENTATION:
- CPU-based contraction using explicit loops
- Real doubles (no complex numbers)
- Column-major tensor indexing
- Adapted from dmrg_with_environments.cpp reference

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR:
- Energy should now converge to E_0 ≈ -3.375 for L=8 Heisenberg chain
- Replaces previous placeholder (E=1.0 constant)

PERFORMANCE:
- CPU contraction is slower than GPU but gets correct physics
- Can be optimized to GPU with hipTensor later (Phase 3)

STATUS: Ready to test real Hamiltonian DMRG on MI300X

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: replace O(n²) duplicate detection with HashMap-based O(n) (#516)

The verify_consistency_of_operations function had two nested loops
scanning all ops pairwise — O(n²) for both duplicate detection and
same-path-key conflict detection. Replace both with single-pass
HashMap counting for O(n) complexity.

Also adds Hash derive to QualifiedGroveDbOp (all inner types already
implement Hash) and removes dead `i == ops_len` guards.

Audit finding P1.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix NIS6 gap calculations and update Claude model

- reconcileN6 now computes gap as sc3-cur instead of summing only
  positive pre-computed e[5] values, ensuring the circle number
  always equals the sum of displayed NIS6 sub-commune gaps
- All 6 NIS6 gap display locations now use sc3-cur (e[3]-e[4])
  instead of pre-computed e[5] which had 153 inconsistent entries
- Update Claude model from deprecated claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  to claude-sonnet-4-6 (fixes 400 API error)

commit.message:
fix(sleep): ne pas afficher "sieste en retard" après saisie manuelle

Lors d'une saisie manuelle avec une heure passée, A4 affichait
systématiquement "Sieste en retard" même si la fenêtre n'était
manquée que de quelques minutes. On track maintenant le meilleur
temps calculé par A1/A2/A3 et on ne qualifie de "retard" que si
la fenêtre idéale était >30 min dans le passé.

commit.message:
feat(mcp-prometheus): add mcp-prometheus base with Valkey (#501)

## Summary

Adds the base Flux resources for deploying mcp-prometheus on management
clusters:

- `extras/mcp-prometheus/namespace.yaml` — `mcp-prometheus` namespace
- `extras/mcp-prometheus/oci-repository.yaml` — OCIRepository for
mcp-prometheus chart
- `extras/mcp-prometheus/valkey-oci-repository.yaml` — OCIRepository for
Valkey chart
- `extras/mcp-prometheus/helm-release.yaml` — HelmRelease (values from
Konfiguration ConfigMap)
- `extras/mcp-prometheus/valkey-helm-release.yaml` — Valkey standalone
HelmRelease (team: atlas)
- `extras/mcp-prometheus/konfiguration.yaml` — Konfiguration referencing
giantswarm-config

Per-installation secrets (oauth-credentials, valkey-credentials) are
managed in giantswarm-management-clusters.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify Flux reconciles all resources on gazelle without errors
- [ ] Verify Valkey pod starts healthy
- [ ] Verify mcp-prometheus HelmRelease installs successfully

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
Fix menu bar label shifting when timer ticks

- Replace dynamic text label (icon + name + time) with a fixed-width
  HStack showing icon + time only (task name removed from menu bar)
- Apply .monospacedDigit() so digit-width changes don't shift layout
- Set .frame(width: 100) so the label never resizes as time updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix lock leak in create_abap_class/program/ddic_table + add lock stealing + prompt fixes

Critical bug: Lock→Write→Unlock pattern had no try/finally, so if the
PUT write request failed or threw, the lock was NEVER released. This left
SAP objects permanently locked (visible in SM12), preventing future edits
and causing the agent to create duplicate classes with different names.

Fixes:
1. All 3 write tools (create_abap_class, create_abap_program, create_ddic_table)
   now use try/finally to GUARANTEE unlock even on errors/exceptions.
2. create_abap_class: Added lock-stealing logic — when lock conflict (409),
   tries LOCK with lockType=STEAL to recover from stale locks left by
   crashed previous runs.
3. Prompt: Added METHOD SIGNATURE RULES — all RETURNING/IMPORTING parameters
   MUST have TYPE declaration ("A RETURNING parameter must be fully typed").
4. Prompt: Strengthened EFFICIENCY RULES — never create second class if first
   has errors, stop if class is locked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

across lines (unlike C/C++). Three Format/g_hDB.Format calls in
ReadyCheck_Decline and Timer_ExpireStaleQueue still had the pattern
"str1 " "str2", causing error 001 at lines 1067, 1076, and 1100.
These were past the 26-error threshold in the first CI run and only
surfaced in the second run. Merged each split string into one line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix React TDZ white screen — move useState declarations before useEffect

The keyboard shortcuts useEffect (line 957) referenced editingItem,
expandedCard, searchTerm, searchRef and their setters in its dependency
array, but all of these were declared via useState/useRef AFTER the
useEffect block (lines 986-998).

This violates React's Rules of Hooks and causes the minifier to assign
the same short name (pe) to both a React scheduler internal variable and
the editingItem state variable, creating a Temporal Dead Zone at runtime:
  "Cannot access 'pe' before initialization"

Fix: move all UI state declarations to before the first useEffect so
every hook can safely reference all state variables in dependency arrays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove Content-Type from GET requests to prevent CORS preflight

apiFetch was unconditionally sending Content-Type: application/json on
every request including GETs. Content-Type is not a CORS simple header,
so browsers fired an OPTIONS preflight before GET /healthz — the very
request used to wake the Render cold-start instance. When Render is
spinning up it cannot answer the preflight, causing ERR_FAILED and the
'flight request does not pass access control check' error.

Fix: only attach Content-Type for methods that carry a body (POST/PUT/PATCH).
Also give getHealth() its own bare fetch with no extra headers so it is
guaranteed to stay a simple CORS request regardless of future apiFetch changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
